channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The 30 most exciting new shows of 2026: TechRadar's top TV picks on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more
item.description: There'll be lots of great new TV shows this year – here are our top choices for early 2026, plus some to look out for in the months ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: Notion isn't just a note-taking app –here are 5 ways I'm using it to run my life in 2026
item.description: Notion has become an all-in-one app like no other – here’s why it’s worth a look in 2026, plus my favorite new tools.
item.dc:creator: Lloyd Coombes, TechRadar

item.title: Big Tech's fast-expanding plans for data centers are running into stiff community opposition
item.description: Tech companies looking to plunge billions of dollars into ever-bigger data centers to power artificial intelligence and cloud computing are increasingly being voted down.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The 14 global trends that will shape the climate in 2026
item.description: The future of the energy transition is being shaped by President Donald Trump’s climate policy rollbacks that favor fossil fuels — but also the life support for renewables and batteries paradoxically coming from data center power demand.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026
item.description: The White House is expected to kick off 2026 with a new national cyber strategy. Here's what else we're watching in a consequential year for cyber. The post Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Grok makes sexual images of kids as users test AI guardrails
item.description: Elon Musk's artificial intelligence chatbot Grok created sexualized images of minors on the social media platform X in response to user prompts in recent days, drawing criticism of a tool that positions itself as less restrained than its competition.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: There's still no sign of the Nvidia RTX 5080 32GB despite rumors – and a fresh claim suggests a worrying possibility
item.description: Fresh rumors suggest Nvidia RTX 5080 32GB was initially set for cloud gaming only, and that's a worrying sign for any potential launch.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Apple Fitness+ is kicking off 2026 with new workout programs
item.description: Apple Fitness+ is kicking off 2026 with new workout programs aimed at encouraging users to jump into – or jump back into – yoga, strength, or HIIT.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Fake AI chat results are spreading dangerous Mac malware
item.description: Mac malware campaign uses fake AI chat results to deliver AMOS through terminal commands, security researchers report. Attackers exploit trust in AI-generated cleanup guides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US cybersecurity experts plead guilty to attacking US companies with ransomware
item.description: Two cybersecurity professionals charged with running a ransomware operation have pleaded guilty to conspiring to obstruct, delay, or affect commerce through extortion. They will be sentenced on March 12, 2026, the US Department of Justice announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI disclosure in healthcare: What patients must know
item.description: States require AI disclosure in healthcare as California, Colorado and Utah implement laws mandating transparency when artificial intelligence influences patient care decisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The complete guide to NotebookLM
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. NotebookLM is the most useful free AI tool of 2025. It has twin superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Never lose your car with Maps parking tools
item.description: Learn how to set up Google Maps and Apple Maps on your phone to automatically remember where you parked. Step-by-step instructions for enabling location services and parking detection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intro to Hotwire: HTML over the wire
item.description: If you’ve been watching the JavaScript landscape for a while, you’ve likely noticed the trend toward simplicity in web application development. An aspect of this trend is leveraging HTML, REST, and HATEOAS (hypermedia as the engine of application state) to do as much work as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI agents 2025 recap: What happened and what to expect next year
item.description: In artificial intelligence, 2025 marked a decisive shift. Systems once confined to research labs and prototypes began to appear as everyday tools. At the center of this transition was the rise of AI agents—AI systems that can use other software tools and act on their own.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Digital surveys may have hit the AI point of no return
item.description: There’s bad news for those using digital surveys to try to understand people’s online behavior: We may no longer be able to determine whether a human is responding to them or not, a recent study has shown—and there seems to be no way around this problem.
item.dc:creator: S.A. Applin, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft typosquatting scam swaps letters to steal logins
item.description: Phishing scammers use rnicrosoft.com domain to impersonate Microsoft by replacing "m" with "rn" in typosquatting attacks that steal login credentials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia licenses Groq’s inferencing chip tech and hires its leaders
item.description: Nvidia has licensed intellectual property from inferencing chip designer Groq, and hired away some of its senior executives, but stopped short of an outright acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI tightens AI rules for teens but concerns remain
item.description: OpenAI announces new teen safety rules for ChatGPT users under 18, blocking romantic roleplay and requiring extra caution on body image topics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIOs’ top resolution should be to create a visions-rich 2026
item.description: Confusion abounds in today’s technology world. In this moment of AI-amplified centrifugal chaos, organizations need the unifying — and synthesizing — focus of understandable and achievable technology visions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Foreign tech workers are avoiding travel to the US
item.description: I go to a lot of tech conferences — 13 in 2025 — and many of those I attend are outside the US; several are in London, one is in Amsterdam, another in Paris, and two in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
